anchors_in_use.name: Bash
anchors_in_use.notes: Pin is a floor (>= 4). README documents that macOS ships 3.2 and recommends Homebrew bash 5+. Script uses bash 4+ syntax: arrays (`local lessons=(...)`), C-style for loops, process substitution, `local`, builtin `clear` (with `|| true` fallback).

anchors_in_use.name: POSIX userland
anchors_in_use.applies_to: external commands used by the script (mkdir, touch, seq, cat, rm, printf, clear)
anchors_in_use.pin_source: AGENTS.md:75-77 ("`bash` and a standard POSIX userland")
anchors_in_use.notes: No specific POSIX revision pinned. The script also uses `head`, `tail`, `wc`, `seq` in lessons (taught content, not script dependencies); `seq` is GNU coreutils on Linux, present on macOS BSD coreutils too.

anchors_in_use.name: ANSI terminal color (NO_COLOR)
anchors_in_use.notes: The script honors the NO_COLOR environment variable per https://no-color.org. ANSI 256-color codes used (`\e[1;38;5;208m` etc.).

anchors_in_use.name: shellcheck (optional)
anchors_in_use.notes: Optional in the contributor workflow; ROADMAP Phase 2 names it as exit criterion but no pass has been recorded. Profile records this as known debt.

anchors_not_applicable.name: OpenAPI Specification
anchors_not_applicable.reason: No HTTP API surface in scope.

anchors_not_applicable.name: JSON Schema
anchors_not_applicable.reason: No structured payload validation surface in scope.

anchors_not_applicable.reason: No MCP server or client surface. Grep returned no matches across tracked files.

anchors_not_applicable.reason: No release/build pipeline; no signed artifact provenance. Profile records build/source provenance as `absent` (open known-debt: release posture).
